prose: Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF backend, density-fit RI) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2). Geometry was the user-supplied fixed .xyz file (no re-optimization). Partial charges were computed via Mulliken population analysis (PySCF). The headline value reported is the maximum f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui index), f⁺ = 0.39938966, located at atom 3 (ring carbon — most susceptible to nucleophilic attack). The most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (carbonyl oxygen), with f⁻ = 0.23839906. All three SCFs (N, N+1, N−1) converged; charge conservation: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. Integrity status: trustworthy = true. No engine warnings were reported. CLI invocation: cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz. Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260702-233756_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
